after_heading: 1. The Elman Recurrence: One State, One Rule, Shared Across Time
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a small determined hiker walking step by step along a winding path through a long landscape, carrying a single tiny overstuffed backpack into which, at every footstep, a fresh little glowing memento of what just happened is crammed while an older faded memento quietly tumbles out the bottom unnoticed, the backpack never growing larger no matter how far the journey stretches. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A small hiker walks a long winding path carrying one tiny fixed-size backpack, stuffing in a fresh glowing memory at each step while an older faded memory falls out the bottom, the bag never growing, depicting the vanilla RNN hidden state as a fixed-width running summary that must overwrite the distant past to make room for the present.
caption: The hidden state is one small bag for an unbounded journey: every step writes something new in, so the oldest memories are the first to fall out.

after_heading: 1. The Long-Term-Dependency Cure: A Separate Cell State
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a long smooth moving walkway running straight across the scene carrying a precious glowing parcel gently forward, with three friendly round-helper characters stationed along it turning three big round dials, the first dial easing some faded old cargo off the walkway, the second dial letting a measured trickle of fresh glowing cargo join the parcel, and the third dial at the far end choosing how much of the glowing cargo is released down a happy little side ramp for the world to see. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A precious glowing parcel rides a long near-frictionless conveyor belt almost untouched while three valve-keepers operate three flow-valves, one draining old cargo, one admitting new cargo, one releasing cargo to the outside, depicting the LSTM cell state as a protected additive carry path controlled by the forget, input, and output gates.
caption: An LSTM is a near-frictionless conveyor for memory: three gate-valves meter what leaves, what enters, and what gets shown, while the carried state rides forward almost undisturbed.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: two friendly cyclists riding side by side at exactly the same speed up a hill, the first towing a heavy three-pannier cargo trailer and the second a trim lighter two-pannier trailer, both reaching the same milestone post together, the lighter rider grinning easily while the heavier rider puffs, making clear the leaner machine keeps pace with noticeably less to carry. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two cyclists climb a hill neck and neck, one hauling a heavy three-pannier trailer and the other a lighter two-pannier trailer, both reaching the same point together, depicting the GRU matching the LSTM's accuracy with two gates and fewer parameters instead of three.
caption: The GRU keeps pace with the LSTM while carrying less: two gates instead of three reach nearly the same finish line at a lower parameter cost.

after_heading: 1. The Seq2Seq Idea: Encode the History, Decode the Horizon
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a busy worker on the left operating a giant funnel-press that squeezes an enormous sprawling scroll of a whole year of wiggling waveform history down into one tiny glowing marble, handing that single marble across a narrow gap to a second worker on the right who carefully unpacks it back out into a fresh unfurling forecast strip, the comically tiny marble straining to contain everything that went in. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A worker squeezes a year-long sprawling waveform scroll through a funnel-press into one tiny glowing marble and hands it to a second worker who unpacks it into a forecast strip, depicting the encoder-decoder bottleneck where the entire input history is compressed into a single fixed context vector before decoding.
caption: The whole input has to fit through one tiny vector: squeeze a year into a single marble, hand it across, and hope the decoder can unpack everything that went in.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: two figures standing on a turning circular treadmill-loop that visibly cycles through a predict-then-correct motion, the figures revealed to be the very same character seen twice, one wearing a tidy engineer's hard hat with fixed dials and one wearing a soft knitted student cap with adjustable knobs, both performing the identical step-forward-then-adjust gesture in perfect sync, making plain they are one mechanism wearing two different hats. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: The same character appears twice on one turning predict-then-correct loop, once in an engineer's hard hat with fixed dials and once in a student cap with adjustable knobs, performing the identical step-and-adjust gesture, depicting the Kalman filter and the gated RNN as one predict-correct mechanism with fixed versus learned, adaptive gain.
caption: The Kalman filter and the gated cell are the same predict-correct loop in different hats: one with its gain handed to it, the other with its gain learned and made adaptive.

after_heading: 1. Architectural Choices: Depth, Direction, and Residuals
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a delighted two-headed reader scanning a long horizontal paper strip from both ends inward at once, one head sweeping left to right and the other right to left meeting happily in the middle, then a sudden look of dawning horror as the reader notices the right-hand portion of the strip is still blank and unwritten because it represents the future that has not happened yet, the backward-reading head caught peeking at pages that do not exist. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A two-headed reader scans a paper strip from both ends inward at once, then recoils in horror on realizing the right half is still blank because it is the unwritten future, depicting bidirectional reading as pure benefit for offline labeling but data leakage when applied to real-time forecasting.
caption: Reading from both ends at once is a gift for offline labeling and a trap for forecasting: the backward pass is happily consulting a future that has not happened yet.
